page: Google Cloud Platform n2d-standard-80 is a general-purpose server instance powered by an AMD EPYC 7B13 processor using the x86_64 architecture. It features 80 virtual CPUs mapped to 40 physical cores with multithreading enabled, providing 2 threads per core. The instance is configured with 320.0 GB of system memory, yielding a balanced allocation of 4.0 GB of RAM per virtual core. It does not include local storage or integrated GPU hardware, and does not bundle complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Benchmark results indicate strong multi-core and memory performance. The server achieves a stress-ng multi-core CPU score of 107,964.98 and a PassMark CPU score of 69,828.6, placing it in the top 25% of tested hardware. Cryptographic capabilities are highly efficient, with the PassMark CPU encryption test scoring in the top 10% at 85,389.14, aided by hardware-accelerated AES and SHA instruction sets. Memory bandwidth is also notable, reaching 5,084.96 GB/sec for cached small-block reads and over 200 GB/sec for uncached large-block reads.

While multi-threaded performance and memory throughput are strong, single-core performance is moderate, with a Geekbench single-core score of 1,506.0 and a stress-ng single-core score of 2,693.39, both falling in the middle 50% tier. Similarly, LLM inference speeds for prompt processing and text generation remain average. Given these characteristics, the instance is highly suited for highly parallelized workloads such as database operations, static web serving, and multi-threaded data processing, where its high core count and memory bandwidth can be fully utilized.
description: Google Cloud Platform n2d-standard-80 is a general-purpose server instance equipped with 80 vCPUs and 320.0 GB of RAM, powered by an AMD EPYC 7B13 processor. Utilizing the x86_64 architecture with 40 physical cores and multithreading, it delivers a balanced 4.0 GB of memory per core. The instance lacks local storage and GPU accelerators. Performance benchmarks show strong multi-core processing and memory bandwidth, with a PassMark CPU score of 69,828.6 and a top-tier encryption score of 85,389.14. However, single-core performance and LLM inference speeds remain average. This resource profile makes the instance highly efficient for parallelized workloads, database operations, and high-throughput web serving rather than single-threaded tasks.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform n2d-standard-80 is an x86_64 server with 80 vCPUs and 320 GB RAM. Powered by AMD EPYC processors, it offers strong multi-core performance and high memory bandwidth.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform n2d-standard-80 features 80 vCPUs, 320 GB RAM, and AMD EPYC processors, delivering strong multi-core performance.
tagline: A high-core general-purpose instance featuring dedicated AMD EPYC processors, balanced memory allocation, and strong multi-threaded performance.
bullet_points: 80 vCPUs and 320.0 GB of system memory powered by AMD EPYC 7B13.
Strong multi-core CPU and memory bandwidth performance in the top 25%.
Top-tier cryptographic performance utilizing hardware-accelerated instruction sets.
No local storage or GPU accelerators included in the configuration.
Best suited for database operations, static web serving, and parallelized workloads.
